action.reasoning: Observation: The predict.py script is still encountering a CUDA error despite my attempts to force CPU usage in the training script. The error occurs when the model tries to make predictions, specifically when converting observations to float on the GPU. This suggests that the loaded model still has GPU tensors even though training was forced to CPU. Analysis: The issue is that when the model was saved after training, it might have retained references to GPU tensors. When loading the model in predict.py, I need to explicitly specify that it should be loaded on CPU. Decision: Update the predict.py script to load the model on CPU using the device='cpu' parameter.
